textRun.content: A comprehensive test of every Google Docs feature
A simple paragraph with no formatting.
A second paragraph separated by a blank line. This one has two sentences.
To be, or not to be, that is the question.
This section tests styles that go beyond basic markdown.

Justified-paragraph-placeholder: Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit. Sed do eiusmod tempor incididunt ut labore et dolore magna aliqua. Ut enim ad minim veniam, quis nostrud exercitation ullamco laboris.
First line followed by a second line in the same paragraph using a soft line break.
A paragraph with multiple sentences. Each sentence on the same line. No special breaks.
Mathematical: α β γ δ π Ω ∞ ± × ÷ ≤ ≥ ≠
This paragraph has a footnote reference (footnotes must be added manually via Google Docs UI, as the API cannot create them programmatically through this tool).
This section contains text that could be linked to via bookmarks (bookmark creation requires direct API access).
A page break should appear after this paragraph (inserted separately).
Headers and footers (must be configured via Google Docs UI)
Page numbers (must be added via Google Docs UI)
Table of contents (must be inserted via Google Docs UI)
Summary of Google Docs features tested
Font family (Arial, Times New Roman, Courier New)
Named styles: Title, Heading 1-6, Normal Text
